Struggling With Pigmentation,
Melasma or Dark Spots?

Let's help you get a clear, even skin tone using dermatologist-led medical treatments, clinical peels, and advanced lasers in Erode.

Book Consultation WhatsApp Us

Consultant Dermatologist | Pigmentation & Melasma Treatment | Laser Treatments Available

The Challenge

Pigmentation and Melasma Can Be Difficult to Treat Without Proper Diagnosis

Many patients struggle with pigmentation, melasma, dark spots or uneven skin tone for years. Many try fairness creams, home remedies and cosmetic treatments but the pigmentation keeps coming back.

Pigmentation and melasma cannot be treated with creams alone in many cases. Proper diagnosis, sun protection, medical treatment and sometimes procedures are required for improvement.

Pigmentation treatment takes time and requires a structured treatment plan, but with proper treatment, pigmentation and skin tone can be significantly improved.

Expert Guidance

Dermatologist-Led Pigmentation and Melasma Treatment

At Dr. Alam’s Skin Clinic, we first identify the type of pigmentation—whether it's melasma, post-inflammatory pigmentation, tanning or other causes.

Based on the diagnosis, we create a personalized treatment plan which may include medical creams, oral medications, chemical peels and laser treatments.

Dr. Howthul Alam Raja is a Consultant Dermatologist trained at **Madras Medical College** and has experience treating melasma, pigmentation and uneven skin tone using medical and laser dermatology treatments.

"Pigmentation treatment requires patience, sun protection and proper follow-up for best results."

The Process

Pigmentation Treatment in 3 Steps

STEP 1

Diagnosis

We identify the type and cause of pigmentation to ensure the treatment targets the root issue.

STEP 2

Treatment Plan

A customized medical treatment, prescription sunscreen, and procedure plan is created for your skin.

STEP 3

Procedures & Follow-Up

Chemical peels, laser treatments and maintenance visits ensure long-term, stable skin tone improvement.

Advanced Therapy

Pigmentation & Melasma Solutions

We utilize a multi-disciplinary approach to target pigment at various skin depths for optimal clearance.

01

Medical Depigmenting Creams

02

03

06

07

Note: Pigmentation treatment usually requires multiple sessions and regular clinical follow-up.

The Future

Imagine Having Clear, Even Skin Tone Again

When pigmentation improves and skin tone becomes more even, many patients feel more confident and comfortable without heavy makeup or filters. Treatment can improve dark spots, melasma patches and overall skin brightness.

Our goal is to improve pigmentation gradually and maintain long-term skin tone improvement.

Clinical Proof

Pigmentation Treatment Results

BeforeAfter Clinical Care

We focus on improving:

  • • Melasma patches
  • • Dark spots & sun damage
  • • Uneven skin tone & dullness
  • • Pigmentation after acne (PIH)
  • • Tanning and environmental damage

Results vary depending on pigmentation type, severity and treatment compliance.

The Risks

What Happens If Pigmentation Is Not Treated Properly?

If pigmentation is not treated properly:

  • Pigmentation may become darker
  • Melasma may spread and deepen
  • Skin tone becomes chronically uneven
  • Recurring pigmentation patches
  • More costly treatments required later
  • Money wasted on ineffective OTC creams

Proper diagnosis and treatment helps control pigmentation and maintain long-term results.

Warning:

Unsupervised use of "fairness" or "depigmenting" creams can sometimes make pigmentation worse. Always consult a dermatologist first.

Common Questions

Frequently Asked Questions

How long does pigmentation treatment take? +
Pigmentation treatment usually takes several months depending on severity and type. Consistent sunscreen use is key to seeing progress.
Can melasma be permanently cured? +
Melasma can be controlled and significantly improved, but it may recur without proper maintenance care and strict sun protection.
Are laser treatments safe for pigmentation? +
Yes, when performed by a qualified dermatologist with proper wavelength settings specifically for Indian skin types.
Is sunscreen necessary for pigmentation? +
Sunscreen is the most critical part of treatment. Without it, procedures and creams will not provide long-lasting results.
Final Step

Book Consultation for Pigmentation & Melasma

If you are struggling with pigmentation, melasma or dark spots, book a consultation for proper diagnosis and a structured, clinical treatment plan.
